results.title: OpenMedLM: prompt engineering can out-perform fine-tuning in medical question-answering with open-source large language models
results.tldr: OpenMedLM, a prompting platform delivering state-of-the-art SOTA performance for OS LLMs on medical benchmarks, shows the first results to date demonstrating the ability of OS foundation models to optimize performance, absent specialized fine-tuning, and highlights medical-specific emergent properties in OS LLMs not documented elsewhere to date.
results.abstract: OpenMedLM, a prompting platform delivering state-of-the-art SOTA performance for OS LLMs on medical benchmarks, shows the first results to date demonstrating the ability of OS foundation models to optimize performance, absent specialized fine-tuning, and highlights medical-specific emergent properties in OS LLMs not documented elsewhere to date.

results.title: Enhancing Few-shot Text-to-SQL Capabilities of Large Language Models: A Study on Prompt Design Strategies
results.tldr: This paper conducts a systematic investigation into different demonstration selection methods and optimal instruction formats for prompting LLMs in the Text-to-SQL task, and presents an analysis of the factors contributing to the success of this strategy.

results.tldr: A retrieval-augmented generative QA model (R-GQA) is proposed for event argument extraction that retrieves the most similar QA pair and augments it as prompt to the current example’s context, then decodes the arguments as answers.

results.title: To Generate or to Retrieve? On the Effectiveness of Artificial Contexts for Medical Open-Domain Question Answering
results.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: MedGENIE is presented, the first generate-then-read framework for multiple-choice question answering in medicine, and its findings reveal that generated passages are more effective than retrieved ones in attaining higher accuracy.
results.abstract: MedGENIE is presented, the first generate-then-read framework for multiple-choice question answering in medicine, and its findings reveal that generated passages are more effective than retrieved ones in attaining higher accuracy.

results.title: API-Assisted Code Generation for Question Answering on Varied Table Structures
results.venue: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
results.tldr: A unified TableQA framework that provides a unified representation for structured tables as multi-index Pandas data frames, uses Python as a powerful querying language, and uses few-shot prompting to translate NL questions into Python programs, which are executable on PandasData frames is introduced.
results.abstract: A unified TableQA framework that provides a unified representation for structured tables as multi-index Pandas data frames, uses Python as a powerful querying language, and uses few-shot prompting to translate NL questions into Python programs, which are executable on PandasData frames is introduced.

results.title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of GPT-4V on Knowledge-Intensive Visual Question Answering
results.tldr: Extensive experiments indicate that GPT-4V demonstrates enhanced explanation generation when using composite images as few-shots; GPT-4V and other MLMs produce severe hallucinations when dealing with world knowledge; and Visual knowledge enhanced training and prompting technicals present potential to improve performance.
results.abstract: Extensive experiments indicate that GPT-4V demonstrates enhanced explanation generation when using composite images as few-shots; GPT-4V and other MLMs produce severe hallucinations when dealing with world knowledge; and Visual knowledge enhanced training and prompting technicals present potential to improve performance.

results.title: Prompt Space Optimizing Few-shot Reasoning Success with Large Language Models
results.tldr: The proposed Prompt Space methodology utilizes text embeddings to obtain basis vectors by matrix decomposition, and then constructs a space for representing all prompts, which significantly outperforms state-of-the-art prompt paradigms on ten public reasoning benchmarks.
results.abstract: The proposed Prompt Space methodology utilizes text embeddings to obtain basis vectors by matrix decomposition, and then constructs a space for representing all prompts, which significantly outperforms state-of-the-art prompt paradigms on ten public reasoning benchmarks.

results.title: MinPrompt: Graph-based Minimal Prompt Data Augmentation for Few-shot Question Answering
results.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: This paper presents MinPrompt, a minimal data augmentation framework for open-domain QA based on an approximate graph algorithm and unsupervised question generation, and shows that MinPrompt is able to achieve comparable or better results than baselines with a high degree of efficiency.
results.abstract: This paper presents MinPrompt, a minimal data augmentation framework for open-domain QA based on an approximate graph algorithm and unsupervised question generation, and shows that MinPrompt is able to achieve comparable or better results than baselines with a high degree of efficiency.

results.title: Investigating Prompting Techniques for Zero- and Few-Shot Visual Question Answering
results.tldr: Light is shed on the intricacies of prompting strategies in VLMs for VQA, emphasizing the synergistic use of captions, templates, and pre-processing to enhance model efficacy.
results.abstract: Light is shed on the intricacies of prompting strategies in VLMs for VQA, emphasizing the synergistic use of captions, templates, and pre-processing to enhance model efficacy.

results.title: MMHQA-ICL: Multimodal In-context Learning for Hybrid Question Answering over Text, Tables and Images
results.tldr: This work proposes a Type-specific In-context Learning Strategy for MMHQA, enabling LLMs to leverage their powerful performance in this task, and is the first to use end-to-end LLM prompting method for this task.
results.abstract: This work proposes a Type-specific In-context Learning Strategy for MMHQA, enabling LLMs to leverage their powerful performance in this task, and is the first to use end-to-end LLM prompting method for this task.

results.title: Enhancing In-Context Learning with Answer Feedback for Multi-Span Question Answering
results.venue: Natural Language Processing and Chinese Computing
results.tldr: This paper proposes a novel way of employing labeled data such that it also informs LLM of some undesired output, by extending demonstration examples with feedback about answers predicted by an off-the-shelf model.
results.abstract: This paper proposes a novel way of employing labeled data such that it also informs LLM of some undesired output, by extending demonstration examples with feedback about answers predicted by an off-the-shelf model.

results.title: Instruction Tuning and CoT Prompting for Contextual Medical QA with LLMs
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Natural Language Processing (ICAHN)
results.tldr: These findings suggest that reasoning-aware prompts are useful, but their benefits are model- and scale-dependent, and that fine-tuning on CoT prompts does not universally enhance performance and may even degrade it for certain larger models.
results.abstract: These findings suggest that reasoning-aware prompts are useful, but their benefits are model- and scale-dependent, and that fine-tuning on CoT prompts does not universally enhance performance and may even degrade it for certain larger models.

results.title: Zero-Shot Question Answering over Financial Documents using Large Language Models
results.tldr: The proposed approach uses novel zero-shot prompts that guide the LLM to encode the required reasoning into a Python program or a domain specific language, thus mitigating the limitations of LLM in performing accurate arithmetic calculations.
results.abstract: The proposed approach uses novel zero-shot prompts that guide the LLM to encode the required reasoning into a Python program or a domain specific language, thus mitigating the limitations of LLM in performing accurate arithmetic calculations.

results.title: MaScQA: A Question Answering Dataset for Investigating Materials Science Knowledge of Large Language Models
results.tldr: This work curates a dataset of 650 challenging questions from the materials domain that require the knowledge and skills of a materials student who has cleared their undergraduate degree and evaluates the performance of GPT-3.5 and G PT-4 models on solving these questions via zero-shot and chain of thought prompting.
results.abstract: This work curates a dataset of 650 challenging questions from the materials domain that require the knowledge and skills of a materials student who has cleared their undergraduate degree and evaluates the performance of GPT-3.5 and G PT-4 models on solving these questions via zero-shot and chain of thought prompting.

results.abstract: The results indicate that the combined use of fine-tuning and prompting techniques, especially when incorporating strategic few-shot selection, produces excellent results on both SciQA and DBLP-QuAD, underscore an urgent need for more challenging benchmarks to better assess model capabilities.

results.title: Zero-Shot Prompting Strategies for Table Question Answering with a Low-Resource Language
results.abstract: <jats:p>This work explores the application of zero-shot prompting strategies for table question answering (TQA) in Portuguese, focusing specifically on the Text2SQL task. This task involves translating questions posed in natural language into Structured Query Language (SQL) queries, which can be executed against a database to answer the original question. Given the popularity of relational databases across various domains, advancements in this field can substantially impact the accessibility and

results.title: Prompt Generate Train (PGT): A framework for few-shot domain adaptation, alignment, and uncertainty calibration of a retriever augmented generation (RAG) model for domain specific open book question-answering.
results.tldr: An aligned, uncertainty calibrated model is produced that is competitive with GPT-4 based in-context retrieval augmented generation in generating relevant answers at lower serving costs and can be integrated into a cascading system such as FRUGAL GPT.
results.abstract: An aligned, uncertainty calibrated model is produced that is competitive with GPT-4 based in-context retrieval augmented generation in generating relevant answers at lower serving costs and can be integrated into a cascading system such as FRUGAL GPT.

results.title: The development and evaluation of agricultural question-answering systems based on large language models
results.tldr: One of the first domain-specific question answering systems powered by LLMs is introduced, tailored for agricultural experts such as engineers and technicians, and presents an innovative approach by creating an infrastructure for smart digital applications in the field of agriculture.
results.abstract: One of the first domain-specific question answering systems powered by LLMs is introduced, tailored for agricultural experts such as engineers and technicians, and presents an innovative approach by creating an infrastructure for smart digital applications in the field of agriculture.

results.title: Context-Aware Few-Shot Learning SPARQL Query Generation from Natural Language on an Aviation Knowledge Graph
results.tldr: This work proposes a prompt-based approach that enables natural language to generate SPARQL queries by leveraging the advanced language capabilities of large language models (LLMs) and constructed prompts that include a natural-language question, relevant contextual information from the domain-specific knowledge graph, and several examples of how the task should be executed.
results.abstract: This work proposes a prompt-based approach that enables natural language to generate SPARQL queries by leveraging the advanced language capabilities of large language models (LLMs) and constructed prompts that include a natural-language question, relevant contextual information from the domain-specific knowledge graph, and several examples of how the task should be executed.

results.title: Domain-Specific Few-Shot Table Prompt Question Answering via Contrastive Exemplar Selection
results.abstract: <jats:p>As a crucial task in natural language processing, table question answering has garnered significant attention from both the academic and industrial communities. It enables intelligent querying and question answering over structured data by translating natural language into corresponding SQL statements. Recently, there have been notable advancements in the general domain table question answering task, achieved through prompt learning with large language models. However, in specific domain
results.tldr: This paper designs a prompt template construction strategy for structured SQL generation that utilizes prompt templates to restructure the input for each test data and standardizes the model output, which can enhance the integrity and validity of generated SQL.

results.title: Analyzing Diagnostic Reasoning of Vision–Language Models via Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought Prompting in Medical Visual Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>Medical Visual Question Answering (MedVQA) lies at the intersection of computer vision, natural language processing, and clinical decision-making, aiming to generate accurate responses from medical images paired with complex inquiries. Despite recent advances in vision–language models (VLMs), their use in healthcare remains limited by a lack of interpretability and a tendency to produce direct, unexplainable outputs. This opacity undermines their reliability in medical settings, where tr

results.title: Few-shot Unified Question Answering: Tuning Models or Prompts?
results.venue: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
results.tldr: The paper provides an exhaustive analysis of two paradigms of tuning, model, and prompts, for unified QA under a low-resource setting, revealing that prompt tuning can perform as well as model tuning in a few-shot setting with a good initialization.
results.abstract: The paper provides an exhaustive analysis of two paradigms of tuning, model, and prompts, for unified QA under a low-resource setting, revealing that prompt tuning can perform as well as model tuning in a few-shot setting with a good initialization.

results.title: Software Defined Vehicle Code Generation: A Few-Shot Prompting Approach
results.tldr: This study proposes using prompts, a common and basic strategy to interact with LLMs and redirect their responses using only system prompts with an appropriate and efficient prompt structure designed using advanced prompt engineering techniques, so that LLMs can be crafted without requiring a training session or access to their base design.
results.abstract: This study proposes using prompts, a common and basic strategy to interact with LLMs and redirect their responses using only system prompts with an appropriate and efficient prompt structure designed using advanced prompt engineering techniques, so that LLMs can be crafted without requiring a training session or access to their base design.

results.title: Exploring the Effectiveness of Large Language Models in Financial Question Answering
results.tldr: The preliminary findings reveal significant distinctions between the two models: Llama2 demonstrates a higher frequency of correct answers, but it is prone to hallucinations, often producing incorrect or incomplete information, and Gemma’s performance is notably inferior, struggling to respond accurately to most queries.
results.abstract: The preliminary findings reveal significant distinctions between the two models: Llama2 demonstrates a higher frequency of correct answers, but it is prone to hallucinations, often producing incorrect or incomplete information, and Gemma’s performance is notably inferior, struggling to respond accurately to most queries.

results.title: PMRC: Prompt-Based Machine Reading Comprehension for Few-Shot Named Entity Recognition
results.tldr: PMRC sequentially insert boundary markers for various entity types into the templates and use these markers as anchors during the inference process to differentiate entity types, and can extract all spans of entity types in one round.
results.abstract: PMRC sequentially insert boundary markers for various entity types into the templates and use these markers as anchors during the inference process to differentiate entity types, and can extract all spans of entity types in one round.

results.tldr: The authors explored the wellbore history of all individual exploration wells drilled in the Norwegian shelf in the NPD database and tested several commercial LLMs for information-retrieval tasks for drilling data using zero-shot in-context learning.

results.title: ECSA: Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting and Few-Shot Generalization in Medical Visual Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>Objective: Medical Visual Question Answering (Med-VQA), a key technology that integrates computer vision and natural language processing to assist in clinical diagnosis, possesses significant potential for enhancing diagnostic efficiency and accuracy. However, its development is constrained by two major bottlenecks: weak few-shot generalization capability stemming from the scarcity of high-quality annotated data and the problem of catastrophic forgetting when continually learning new kno
results.tldr: This paper proposes a novel Evolvable Clinical-Semantic Alignment (ECSA) framework, built upon powerful pre-trained vision and language models, with two innovative modules at its core, which validate the framework’s substantial potential for building robust and evolvable clinical decision support systems.

results.title: Domain-Specific Embedding Models for Hydrology and Environmental Sciences: Enhancing Semantic Retrieval and Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong performance across natural language processing tasks, yet their general-purpose embeddings often fall short in domains with specialized terminology and complex syntax, such as hydrology and environmental science. This study introduces HydroEmbed, a suite of open-source sentence embedding models fine-tuned for four QA formats: multiple-choice (MCQ), true/false (TF), fill-in-the-blank (FITB), and open-ended questions. Models were trained on th

results.title: A Role-Aware Multi-Agent Framework for Financial Education Question Answering with LLMs
results.tldr: A multi-agent framework that leverages role-based prompting to enhance performance on domain-specific QA and offers insights for further research in multi-agent financial LLM systems is presented.
results.abstract: A multi-agent framework that leverages role-based prompting to enhance performance on domain-specific QA and offers insights for further research in multi-agent financial LLM systems is presented.

results.title: Few-Shot Multilingual Open-Domain QA from Five Examples
results.venue: Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics
results.tldr: This work introduces a few-shot learning approach to synthesize large-scale multilingual data from large language models (LLMs) and shows the final model, FsModQA, significantly outperforms existing few-shot and supervised baselines in MLODQA and cross-lingual and monolingual retrieval.
results.abstract: This work introduces a few-shot learning approach to synthesize large-scale multilingual data from large language models (LLMs) and shows the final model, FsModQA, significantly outperforms existing few-shot and supervised baselines in MLODQA and cross-lingual and monolingual retrieval.

results.title: PLHF: Prompt Optimization with Few-Shot Human Feedback
results.tldr: PLHF is presented, a few-shot prompt optimization framework inspired by the well-known RLHF technique that employs a specific evaluator module acting as the metric to estimate the output quality.
results.abstract: PLHF is presented, a few-shot prompt optimization framework inspired by the well-known RLHF technique that employs a specific evaluator module acting as the metric to estimate the output quality.

results.title: Agent Fine-Tuning Through Distillation for Domain-Specific LLMs in Microdomains
results.tldr: This paper fine-tuned LLMs using JP1-specific datasets derived from domain manuals and distilled reasoning trajectories generated by LLMs themselves, enhancing decision making accuracy and search efficiency within Hitachi's JP1 middleware.
results.abstract: This paper fine-tuned LLMs using JP1-specific datasets derived from domain manuals and distilled reasoning trajectories generated by LLMs themselves, enhancing decision making accuracy and search efficiency within Hitachi's JP1 middleware.

results.title: Schema Retrieval for Korean Geographic Knowledge Base Question Answering Using Few-Shot Prompting
results.abstract: <jats:p>Geographic Knowledge Base Question Answering (GeoKBQA) has garnered increasing attention for its ability to process complex geographic queries. This study focuses on schema retrieval, a critical step in GeoKBQA that involves extracting relevant schema items (classes, relations, and properties) to generate accurate operational queries. Current GeoKBQA studies primarily rely on rule-based approaches for schema retrieval. These predefine words or descriptions for each schema item. This rule

results.title: Cognitive Reasoning State Machine for Few-Shot Visual Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>The theory of psychological cognition divides reasoning process of the visual question answering into different states, including seeing, imagining, drawing and answering, which corresponds to understanding, retrieving, reasoning and answering four procedures respectively. Inspired by this, we proposed a Cognitive Reasoning State Machine (CRSM) for few-shot visual question answering, which comprises four modules corresponding to the above reasoning process and realizes effectively cross-

results.title: Chain-of-query prompting pipeline for improving small-scale language models in multi-hop open-domain question answering
results.abstract: <jats:p xml:lang="en">[EMBARGOED UNTIL 08/01/2025] Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown to exhibit robust performance in multi-hop open-domain question-answering (ODQA), which is often attributed to the large number of parameters and extensive training. While smaller-scale language models (LMs) offer a more cost-effective approach for real-world applications, these LMs are often challenged with maintaining factual responses in multi-hop ODQA settings. In this thesis, we introduce a novel prom

results.title: Cross-modal Bigraph Mutual Inference Network for Episodic Few-shot Visual Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>Current episodic few-shot Visual Question Answering (VQA) methods usually lack in-depth continuous cross-modal reasoning  between visual and text modalities for effective answer prediction. To address this issue, we propose a novel Cross-modal Bigraph mutual Inference Network (CBIN) for more accurate episodic few-shot VQA,in which a Bigraph mutual feature Distribution Calibration  Module (BDCM) is proposed to realize recursive cross-modal calibration process by cyclical multi-modal featu

results.title:
Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Language Models for
Burmese Domain-Specific Question Answering
results.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in multilingual commonsense reasoning. However, their reliability drastically degrades when deployed in low-resource, domain-specific settings, often resulting in factual fabrications known as hallucinations. This vulnerability is especially acute for the Burmese language, due to its structural complexities, tokenization artifacts, and the dearth of high-quality localized datasets. In this pa

results.title: KGE: Expert Model-Based Domain-Specific Question Answering Method
results.abstract: <jats:p>Domain-specific question answering has long been a major challenge in artificial intelligence. Existing methods, whether based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) or knowledge graph question answering, often lack domain specialization and the ability to explore deep semantics. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel method called KGE, which achieves joint exploration of shallow and deep semantics through a "Keyword + Expert Model" framework. Our approach first extracts s

results.title: Exploring the Capabilities and Limitations of Small Language Models for Domain-Specific Tasks: A case study in Biomedical Question Answering
results.abstract: <jats:p>Recent advances in large transformer-based language models have yielded impressive performance on biomedical question answering, yet their computational cost and model size hinder deployment in resource constrained environments. This paper investigates whether a small, recurrent, domain-specific language model, built from bottom up and trained on curated biomedical corpora, can effectively model healthcare language and generalize to biomedical QA. We design a complete pipeline including

results.venue: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
results.tldr: This work proposes Expert Question Decomposition (EQD), an approach designed to balance the use of domain knowledge with computational efficiency, and evaluates EQD in the financial domain, characterized by specialized knowledge and complex quantitative reasoning, across four benchmark datasets.
results.abstract: This work proposes Expert Question Decomposition (EQD), an approach designed to balance the use of domain knowledge with computational efficiency, and evaluates EQD in the financial domain, characterized by specialized knowledge and complex quantitative reasoning, across four benchmark datasets.

results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Digital Ethics (ICAIDE)

results.title: SAGE: Tool-Augmented LLM Task Solving Strategies in Scalable Multi-Agent Environments
results.tldr: This paper presents SAGE, a specialized conversational AI interface based on the OPACA framework for tool discovery and execution, and implemented a number of task-solving strategies, making use of agentic concepts and prompting methods in various degrees of complexity.
results.abstract: This paper presents SAGE, a specialized conversational AI interface based on the OPACA framework for tool discovery and execution, and implemented a number of task-solving strategies, making use of agentic concepts and prompting methods in various degrees of complexity.

results.title: Comparing Large Language Models on Scrum Certification-Style Questions: Accuracy, Stability, and Error Patterns
results.tldr: Evaluating the performance of three contemporary LLMs in answering 993 Scrum certification-style questions aligned with the Professional Scrum Master I (PSM I) assessment format revealed clear differences among models, with Gemini 3 Flash achieving the highest accuracy, followed by GPT-5 mini and DeepSeek Chat 3.2, while intra-model variability remained low across all conditions.
results.abstract: Evaluating the performance of three contemporary LLMs in answering 993 Scrum certification-style questions aligned with the Professional Scrum Master I (PSM I) assessment format revealed clear differences among models, with Gemini 3 Flash achieving the highest accuracy, followed by GPT-5 mini and DeepSeek Chat 3.2, while intra-model variability remained low across all conditions.

results.title: PARSE: An Open-Domain Reasoning Question Answering Benchmark for Persian
results.tldr: PARSE is introduced, the first open-domain Persian reasoning QA benchmark, containing 10,800 questions across Boolean, multiple-choice, and factoid formats, with diverse reasoning types, difficulty levels, and answer structures, and it is shown that Persian prompts and structured prompting improve performance.
results.abstract: PARSE is introduced, the first open-domain Persian reasoning QA benchmark, containing 10,800 questions across Boolean, multiple-choice, and factoid formats, with diverse reasoning types, difficulty levels, and answer structures, and it is shown that Persian prompts and structured prompting improve performance.

results.title: Few-Shot Multilingual Open-Domain QA from 5 Examples
results.tldr: A few-shot learning approach to synthesise large-scale multilingual data from large language models (LLMs) and can be extended for effective zero-shot adaptation to new languages through a cross-lingual prompting strategy, making it a general and applicable solution for MLODQA tasks without costly large-scale annotation.
results.abstract: A few-shot learning approach to synthesise large-scale multilingual data from large language models (LLMs) and can be extended for effective zero-shot adaptation to new languages through a cross-lingual prompting strategy, making it a general and applicable solution for MLODQA tasks without costly large-scale annotation.

results.title: Evaluating LLMs for Zeolite Synthesis Event Extraction (ZSEE): A Systematic Analysis of Prompting Strategies
results.tldr: It is demonstrated that while LLMs achieve high-level understanding, precise extraction of experimental parameters requires domain-adapted models, providing quantitative benchmarks for scientific information extraction.
results.abstract: It is demonstrated that while LLMs achieve high-level understanding, precise extraction of experimental parameters requires domain-adapted models, providing quantitative benchmarks for scientific information extraction.

results.title: sonrobok4 at Iberlef 2025 - PRESTA: Leveraging LLMs for Text-to-Python Question Answering over Tabular Data in Spanish
results.tldr: This paper presents the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) for text-to-code generation, focusing on text-to-Python approaches to handle diverse question types, and employs a multi-prompt strategy that emphasizes structured table understanding, language-aware prompt construction.
results.abstract: This paper presents the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) for text-to-code generation, focusing on text-to-Python approaches to handle diverse question types, and employs a multi-prompt strategy that emphasizes structured table understanding, language-aware prompt construction.

results.title: Small vs. Large Language Models: A Comparative Study on Multiple-Choice Question Answering in Portuguese
results.venue: Brazilian Symposium on Multimedia and the Web
results.tldr: Performance scales with size but depends on prompting—per-option configurations reduce the SLM–LLM gap, and positional bias is measurable via BPC; future work includes multi-shuffle BPC estimation, calibration, and log-likelihood baselines for per-option scoring and extensions to additional domains and languages.
results.abstract: Performance scales with size but depends on prompting—per-option configurations reduce the SLM–LLM gap, and positional bias is measurable via BPC; future work includes multi-shuffle BPC estimation, calibration, and log-likelihood baselines for per-option scoring and extensions to additional domains and languages.

results.title: Prompt-Enhanced Question Answering for Clinical Texts Using Instruction-Tuned Language Models
results.venue: 2025 1st IEEE Uttar Pradesh Section Women in Engineering International Conference on Electrical Electronics and Computer Engineering (UPWIECON)

results.title: LLMs to Support a Domain Specific Knowledge Assistant
results.tldr: There is no publicly available question-answer dataset in this domain, which has impeded the development of a high-quality chatbot to support companies with IFRS reporting, so a custom evaluation framework is developed to assess question and answer quality across multiple dimensions.
results.abstract: There is no publicly available question-answer dataset in this domain, which has impeded the development of a high-quality chatbot to support companies with IFRS reporting, so a custom evaluation framework is developed to assess question and answer quality across multiple dimensions.

results.title: Construction of a Tomato Disease Knowledge Graph Based on Few-Shot Learning Enhanced by Generative Pre-Trained Transformers
results.venue: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Intelligent Manufacturing (AIIM)

results.title: A Transferable Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Vertical-Domain Question Answering: From Academic Competitions to Cultural Tourism and Financial Technology
results.venue: Advances in Computer Signals and Systems
results.tldr: Experiments demonstrate that the transferable Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework improves answer accuracy, evidence recall, and citation consistency under both in-domain evaluation and few-shot transfer settings, indicating strong transferability and practical potential for deployable vertical-domain question answering systems.
results.abstract: Experiments demonstrate that the transferable Retrieval-Augmented Generation framework improves answer accuracy, evidence recall, and citation consistency under both in-domain evaluation and few-shot transfer settings, indicating strong transferability and practical potential for deployable vertical-domain question answering systems.

results.title: CQARE: Contrastive Question-Answering for Few-shot Relation Extraction with Prompt Tuning
results.tldr: A Contrastive Question-Answering 013 method with prompt tuning for few-shot RE 014 (CQARE), which can provide more robust initialization with prompt 020 tuning while maintaining semantic consistency with the proposed PLM and can effectively avoid label words mapping by reformulating RE as contrastive question an-024 swering.
results.abstract: A Contrastive Question-Answering 013 method with prompt tuning for few-shot RE 014 (CQARE), which can provide more robust initialization with prompt 020 tuning while maintaining semantic consistency with the proposed PLM and can effectively avoid label words mapping by reformulating RE as contrastive question an-024 swering.

results.title: Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering System Using Multimodal Deep Learning
results.tldr: A versatile, resilient, and domain-independent framework that improves LLM-powered Visual Question Answering (VQA) systems by incorporating structured reasoning and external knowledge and greatly enhances explainability and reliability, making it especially appropriate for medical VQA applications.
results.abstract: A versatile, resilient, and domain-independent framework that improves LLM-powered Visual Question Answering (VQA) systems by incorporating structured reasoning and external knowledge and greatly enhances explainability and reliability, making it especially appropriate for medical VQA applications.

results.title: Evaluating the Fine-Tuning Techniques for Improving the Accuracy of GPT-3 in Domain-Specific Applications
